note: Self-built digital judgment gold set. Source: existence-only axe rules that PASS on present-but-poor values (image-alt / link-name / frame-title), surfaced as quality-review judgment findings (AxeBucket.PASSES). Each item is labelled from the element snippet ALONE — the same input the drafter and judge receive, no page context. Conformance is binary: supports = the value adequately serves its success criterion; does_not_support = present but inadequate (a quality failure axe cannot decide). Borderline calls are flagged in notes — single-labeller honesty; kappa is judge-vs-this-one-labeller. SC assignment, spot-checked against WCAG Understanding: image-alt -> 1.1.1; link-name -> 2.4.4 (the accessible name EXISTS, so 4.1.2 Name-Role-Value is satisfied — the residual defect is link purpose, 2.4.4); frame-title -> 4.1.2 (the frame's accessible-name quality; no dedicated purpose SC exists for frames). gold_severity is informational and per-category (the judge does not score severity). finding_id is not stored: it hashes an absolute file:// URL, so it is derived at load time by matching each item's target to the scanned finding. Both conformance polarities are present so a balanced calibration draft set is constructible.

items.notes: raw camera filename; conveys nothing about the image
generic word; no specific description of the product shown

items.value: Frequency response graph: flat from 20 Hz to 20 kHz
items.notes: names the figure type and its key data — an equivalent for the chart
borderline; states only that it is a team photo, identifies no one and adds no article-relevant information
for a logo the organisation name is the accepted text alternative (WCAG logo technique)
generic; a data figure carrying a trend must have that trend described

items.value: Golden Gate Bridge at sunset, half-wrapped in fog
items.notes: names the subject and a distinguishing detail
generic word; no description
describes the interaction, not the image content; not a text alternative
classic non-descriptive link text; purpose not conveyed
borderline; the topic is not conveyed by the link text alone
purpose and format fully described
non-descriptive; purpose not conveyed
purpose clear from the text alone
borderline; single word, purpose (details of what?) unclear from the text alone
borderline; a raw URL as link text — the path is readable but it is not human-friendly link purpose
purpose clear
borderline; the object (download what?) is not conveyed
generic; does not describe the frame's content
borderline; names the media type, not the content (video of what?)

items.value: Product demo: setting up your account
items.notes: describes the frame's content
generic; describes nothing
borderline; names the type, not the content (map of what?)

items.value: Map of our San Francisco office location
items.notes: describes the frame's content
generic; describes nothing
borderline; names the frame's content/purpose adequately — a user knows it is an ad
describes the frame's content
